description:
This composite diagram illustrates key concepts of electromagnetic wave polarization and scattering.

Section (i), titled "Locus of an elliptically polarized wave", shows an electric field propagating along the z-axis, with its vector tracing a red elliptical spiral in 3D space defined by x, y, and z axes. A "Transverse plane" perpendicular to the z-axis shows the elliptical trace of the electric field vector at a specific point in time. The propagation direction of the electric field is indicated by a dashed arrow along the z-axis.

Section (ii), titled "Typical polarizations", presents three 2D illustrations of common polarization states on an x-y coordinate system:
(a) "Horizontal polarization, Vertical polarization": Shows horizontal (H) and vertical (V) linear polarizations aligned with the x and y axes, respectively.
(b) "Linear 45 degree polarization, Linear -45 degree polarization": Depicts linear polarizations at +45° and -45° angles relative to the x-axis.
(c) "Left circular polarization, Right circular polarization": Illustrates circular polarizations, with "L" indicating counter-clockwise (Left) and "R" indicating clockwise (Right) rotation.

Section (iii), titled "Scattering with respect to polarization", demonstrates how different polarization states scatter off a target, specifically a group of evergreen trees on ground:
(a) "HH": Shows horizontally (H) polarized incident waves (wavy lines with H) scattering as horizontally (H) polarized waves (red arrows and wavy lines with H) from the trees and ground, representing a co-polarization (Horizontal transmit, Horizontal receive) scenario.
(b) "HV and VH": Shows horizontally (H) polarized incident waves (wavy lines with H) scattering as vertically (V) polarized waves (green arrows and wavy lines with V) from the trees, representing a cross-polarization (Horizontal transmit, Vertical receive) scenario.
(c) "VV": Shows vertically (V) polarized incident waves (wavy lines with V) scattering as vertically (V) polarized waves (blue arrows and wavy lines with V) from the trees and ground, representing a co-polarization (Vertical transmit, Vertical receive) scenario.
